# AGENT_CONTEXT — @brandos/ai-runtime-layer

---

## Package Purpose

`@brandos/ai-runtime-layer` is the **sole AI execution engine** for the BrandOS platform.

Every AI invocation — cloud or local, streaming or batch — flows through this package's `llmRouter` singleton. No other package may invoke AI providers directly.

**Responsibility boundary:** This package owns provider SDK calls, retry/fallback/circuit-breaker logic, telemetry recording, capability detection, and prompt assembly. It does NOT own orchestration decisions, governance rules, artifact compilation, or persistence.

---

## Public Interfaces

### Primary call surface (apps/web routes, agents, control-plane-layer)

```typescript
import {
  callWithMode,     // Primary: call AI runtime, never throws, returns RouterResult
  callLLM,          // Simplified: throws on failure instead of UnavailableResponse
  isUnavailable,    // Type guard: RouterResult → UnavailableResponse
  setRuntimeConfigProvider,  // Wire admin config at startup (call once)
  ensureRuntimeInitialized,  // Idempotent version of setRuntimeConfigProvider
  resetRuntime,              // Invalidate singleton after admin provider changes
  getAvailableModels,        // Static model list for UI (not live health)
  engineLabel,               // Attribution badge string
  getActiveTelemetryStats,
  getActiveTelemetryHistory,
} from '@brandos/ai-runtime-layer'
```

### Type-only interface imports (for mocks, DI, test doubles)

```typescript
import type { IAIRuntime } from '@brandos/ai-runtime-layer'
import type { IAIRuntimeRequirement } from '@brandos/ai-runtime-layer/src/interfaces/IAIRuntimeRequirement'
```

### VLM (vision language model)

```typescript
import { analyzeImageWithVLM, analyzeMultipleImages, checkBrandCompliance } from '@brandos/ai-runtime-layer'
```

### Backward-compat alias

```typescript
// LLMTaskType is an alias for TaskType — use TaskType in new code
import type { LLMTaskType } from '@brandos/ai-runtime-layer'
```

---

## Package Requirements

### Runtime requirements

| Requirement | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| At least one provider configured | ENV or admin settings | Without providers, all runs return `terminal_failure` |
| `setRuntimeConfigProvider()` called at startup | `apps/web/instrumentation.ts` | Must be called before first `callWithMode()` |
| Node.js ≥ 22 (24.x recommended, see .nvmrc) | package.json engines | Native fetch (≥18) and native WebSocket (≥22, used by IntelligenceOS's Supabase client) both required platform-wide |

### Environment variables (loaded by `ConfigLoader.fromEnv()`)

| Variable | Provider | Required |
|---|---|---|
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | openai | Optional |
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | anthropic | Optional |
| `GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY` | google | Optional |
| `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` | deepseek | Optional |
| `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` | ollama | Optional (defaults to localhost:11434) |
| `LMSTUDIO_BASE_URL` | lmstudio | Optional (defaults to localhost:1234) |
| Any key defined in `PROVIDER_REGISTRY` with protocol `openai-compatible` | auto | Optional |

At least one must be set, or admin settings must configure at least one enabled provider.

---

## State Ownership

### Module-level singletons (live for process lifetime)

| Singleton | Location | Survives `resetRuntime()` | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `_runtime` | `llmRouter.ts` | No — replaced | Active `AIRuntimeAdapter` instance |
| `_configProvider` | `llmRouter.ts` | Yes | Admin config injection function |
| `_sharedCircuitBreaker` | `config/factory.ts` | Yes | Per-provider failure tracking |
| `_sharedRateLimiter` | `config/factory.ts` | Yes | Token budget tracking |
| `_sharedCostTracker` | `config/factory.ts` | Yes | Cumulative cost tracking |

### Hidden state agents must know

- **Circuit breaker state** persists across `resetRuntime()`. If a provider was failing before a config reload, it remains in OPEN state.
- **Telemetry history** is lost on `resetRuntime()` — `TelemetryEngine` is recreated. Stats API resets to zero after a runtime rebuild.
- **Capability cache** (60s TTL) is per `CapabilityEngine` instance. Rebuilding clears it.
- **`_configProvider`** defaults to a no-op (`() => ({})`) until `setRuntimeConfigProvider()` is called.

---

## Allowed Imports

```
@brandos/contracts      ← AI runtime interfaces, artifact types, TaskType
@brandos/shared-utils   ← Logger, withRetry, CircuitBreaker, RateLimiter, CostTracker
@brandos/runtime-config ← ProviderSettings, toAIRuntimeConfig, RuntimeConfig
```

---

## Forbidden Imports (ENFORCED by boundary scripts)

```
@brandos/output-control-layer  — RULE-4 (Fix C1): artifact prompts via globalThis bridge only
@brandos/governance-layer      — governance is downstream, not upstream
@brandos/brand-intelligence    — brand data is injected via contract, not imported
@brandos/control-plane-layer   — CPL calls this package, not the reverse
@brandos/artifact-engine-layer — engine calls this package, not the reverse
@brandos/auth                  — no auth needed at execution layer
react, next                    — server-side only package
```

---

## Internal Architecture

```
src/
  index.ts                          ← PUBLIC API barrel
  AIRuntimeAdapter.ts               ← IAIRuntime implementation (core engine)
  llmRouter.ts                      ← singleton, callWithMode, config provider
  vlmService.ts                     ← VLM image analysis
  registry.ts                       ← MODEL_REGISTRY static metadata
  generationModes.ts                ← generation mode definitions
  config/
    factory.ts                      ← factory for runtime instances + shared state
    loader.ts                       ← env-based config loading
  contracts/
    provider.ts                     ← internal provider contract types
  public/
    IAIRuntime.ts                   ← IAIRuntime public interface (type-only)
    IProviderAdapter.ts             ← IProviderAdapter public interface
  interfaces/
    IAIRuntimeRequirement.ts        ← requirements spec interface
  plugins/
    index.ts                        ← plugin registry
  policy-engine/
    index.ts                        ← policy enforcement (safety, cost limits)
  profiles/
    openaiProfile.ts                ← OpenAI model profiles
    deepseekProfile.ts
    groqProfile.ts
    genericOpenAIProfile.ts
    index.ts
  provider-adapters/
    anthropic/index.ts
    openai/index.ts
    google/index.ts
    deepseek/index.ts
    ollama/index.ts
    lmstudio/index.ts
    openai-compatible/index.ts
    openai-compatible/definitions.ts
  gateway/
    index.ts                        ← AIRuntimeGateway (standalone HTTP server mode)
  router-engine/
    index.ts                        ← routing strategy, tier selection, fallback
  runtime-engine/
    index.ts                        ← request execution, retry, streaming
    prompt-builder.ts               ← BuiltPrompt construction
    logger.ts
    resilience.ts
    streaming.ts
  telemetry-engine/
    index.ts                        ← TelemetryEngine, TelemetrySnapshot
  validator-engine/
    index.ts                        ← ValidatorEngine, safety checks
  capability-registry/
    index.ts                        ← capability detection + caching
  utils/
    normalizeError.ts
  __tests__/
    executionEngine.test.ts
    IAIRuntime.contract.test.ts
    llmRouter.test.ts
    MockProviderAdapter.ts
    MockTelemetryEngine.ts
    singleton.integration.test.ts
```

### Key execution flow

```
callWithMode(mode, request)
  → llmRouter._getOrBuildRuntime()       (singleton, rebuilt after resetRuntime())
  → RouterEngine.plan(request)           (select tiers, fallback chain)
  → RuntimeEngine.execute(plan)          (prompt-builder + provider call)
      → PolicyEngine.check()             (safety gate)
      → ProviderAdapter.invoke()         (SDK call)
      → ValidatorEngine.validate()       (output safety)
  → TelemetryEngine.record()            (async, non-blocking)
  → RouterResult                        (LLMResponse | UnavailableResponse)
```

---

## Invariants

**I-1 — Single AI execution pathway.** No other package calls LLM SDKs directly. `callWithMode()` is the only permitted entry point.

**I-2 — `resetRuntime()` does not destroy shared infrastructure.** Circuit breaker, rate limiter, and cost tracker persist across resets. Only the `AIRuntimeAdapter` instance is replaced.

**I-3 — `setRuntimeConfigProvider()` called once at startup.** `apps/web/instrumentation.ts` calls this. It must be called before the first `callWithMode()`. `ensureRuntimeInitialized()` is the idempotent variant.

**I-4 — No imports from OCL.** Artifact task prompts are registered at bootstrap via `globalThis.__brandos_runtime_adapter.registerArtifactPrompt()` (called from `artifact-engine-layer/bootstrap.ts`). This is the Fix C1 resolution.

**I-5 — `UnavailableResponse` is never thrown.** `callWithMode()` returns a `RouterResult` union — callers check `isUnavailable()` rather than catching exceptions. Only `callLLM()` throws.

**I-6 — Telemetry is fire-and-forget.** `TelemetryEngine.record()` must never block the call path. Telemetry failures must be caught and swallowed.

---

## Safe Changes

- Adding a new provider adapter under `src/provider-adapters/<provider>/`
- Adding new models to `registry.ts` `MODEL_REGISTRY`
- Adding new capability checks to `capability-registry/index.ts`
- Bug fixes to existing provider adapters (with regression tests)
- Adding new exports to `src/index.ts`

### Adding a new provider adapter

1. Create `src/provider-adapters/<provider>/index.ts` implementing `IProviderAdapter`
2. Register in `config/factory.ts` provider map
3. Add model profiles to `src/profiles/` if needed
4. Add ENV variable to `config/loader.ts`
5. Add to `PROVIDER_REGISTRY` in `@brandos/contracts`

---

## Dangerous Changes

- Modifying `llmRouter.ts` singleton lifecycle (affects all running requests)
- Changing `RouterResult` union type (breaks all callers using `isUnavailable()`)
- Changing `callWithMode()` call signature (platform-wide breaking change)
- Modifying `config/factory.ts` shared infrastructure (circuit breaker, rate limiter)
- Modifying `AIRuntimeAdapter.ts` core execution (requires full test suite pass)
- Changing `_configProvider` behavior (affects admin config propagation)

---

## Test Strategy

**Test runner:** Vitest (vitest.config.ts)  
**Location:** `src/__tests__/`

Required coverage:
- `IAIRuntime.contract.test.ts` — contract compliance: all interface methods present and behave correctly
- `llmRouter.test.ts` — singleton lifecycle, resetRuntime, config provider wiring
- `executionEngine.test.ts` — retry, fallback, circuit breaker interaction
- `singleton.integration.test.ts` — full call path with `MockProviderAdapter`

**Mock infrastructure:**
- `MockProviderAdapter.ts` — injectable test double for IProviderAdapter
- `MockTelemetryEngine.ts` — injectable test double for ITelemetryEngine

---

## Known Technical Debt

- `globalThis.__brandos_runtime_adapter` bridge is the Fix C1 workaround. This will be cleaned up after Phase 1.1 runtime refactor when `AIRuntimeAdapter` exposes `registerArtifactPrompt()` as a proper method.
- `LLMTaskType` alias is kept for backward compat. New code must use `TaskType`.
- VLM service (`vlmService.ts`) has no dedicated unit tests.

---

## Current Migration Status

### Fix C1 (Complete)
Previously, `@brandos/ai-runtime-layer` imported `ARTIFACT_TASK_PROMPTS` from `@brandos/output-control-layer`. This was a boundary violation (L3b importing from L3b peer).

**Resolution:** Task prompts are now pushed to the runtime at bootstrap via `globalThis.__brandos_runtime_adapter.registerArtifactPrompt()` called from `artifact-engine-layer/bootstrap.ts`. The import from OCL is removed.

---

## Agent Instructions

Before modifying this package:

1. Read this file entirely.
2. Read `src/public/IAIRuntime.ts` — the public interface contract.
3. Read `src/public/IProviderAdapter.ts` — if adding a new provider.
4. Do NOT add any `@brandos/output-control-layer` import — RULE-4 violation.
5. After modifying, run `pnpm test` and `pnpm typecheck` in this package.
6. Run `node scripts/check-boundaries.mjs` from repo root.
7. Update singletons documentation if any `factory.ts` state changes.
